message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature: extract normalize_image_bytes helper in types.py (ImageBlock.image_to_base64 refactor). Only touching types.py. What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My task: add get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor method to ImageBlock class in types.py (around lines 51-110). This is the only file my task requires. If your task also touches types.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can incorporate your change too.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: OK, we both need types.py - unavoidable overlap, that's fine per instructions. To minimize conflicts: I will only touch the image_to_base64 model_validator body (current lines ~67-84) plus add a new helper function normalize_image_bytes (module-level, placed just above class ImageBlock). I will NOT touch resolve_image or add any new methods to ImageBlock. Please place your get_image_mimetype() method elsewhere in the class (e.g. after resolve_image) so our edits don't collide. I'll commit+push first; you merge/rebase on top, or vice versa - whoever finishes first pushes, other merges. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Ack: we both need types.py, changes are adjacent but distinct. My plan: add new method get_image_mimetype(self) on ImageBlock that lazily computes+caches self.image_mimetype via filetype.guess(self.image), and change image_to_base64's mimetype-guessing block (currently 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None') to just call self.get_image_mimetype(). I will NOT touch the base64-encoding line (self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)) or introduce any normalize_image_bytes helper - that's yours. We'll each commit to our own branch and merge via git afterward per the submission protocol; if git merge conflicts on the shared method body, whoever merges will hand-resolve keeping both changes. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Clarifying the split within image_to_base64 body since we're both touching it: I will ONLY change the mimetype-guessing lines (currently: 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None') to a single call 'self.get_image_mimetype()'. You take the base64-encoding line ('self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)') and wrap it in your normalize_image_bytes helper. I will add my new get_image_mimetype() method after resolve_image, as you suggested, and won't touch resolve_image or add normalize_image_bytes. This way our diffs touch different lines in the same function - should merge cleanly or with a trivial manual resolve. Sound good? Proceeding to implement on this basis.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] module-level helper above ImageBlock class, and refactored image_to_base64 to use it. Only touched: import binascii, new function, and body of image_to_base64 (lines ~67-84 originally). Did not touch resolve_image or add new ImageBlock methods. Tests pass (12/12 in tests/base/llms/test_types.py). Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Ack - my implementation is already complete (crossed messages). Final diff: added binascii import, added module-level normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] just above 'class ImageBlock', and image_to_base64 body is now: 'if not self.image: return self' / 'encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)' / 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None' / 'self.image = encoded_image' / 'return self'. So I DID touch the mimetype-guessing line too (changed self.image -> decoded_for_guessing) since normalize_image_bytes needs to run before guessing to handle pre-encoded input correctly. When you merge, please adapt your get_image_mimetype() to accept/use decoded_for_guessing (or call normalize_image_bytes internally) rather than raw self.image, otherwise mimetype guessing will break for pre-encoded base64 images. Happy to hand-resolve the merge together if useful.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented get_image_mimetype() as a new ImageBlock method placed after resolve_image. Changed the model_validator image_to_base64 body to call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of inlining the filetype.guess logic (didn't touch the base64.b64encode line - that's yours). All 12 existing tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. Pushed to team/agent2. Let me know when you've pushed your normalize_image_bytes change and I'll merge team/agent1 in.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1, resolved conflict: image_to_base64 now calls normalize_image_bytes() for encoding + self.get_image_mimetype() for mimetype (instead of inlining filetype.guess), and get_image_mimetype() now uses normalize_image_bytes() internally to decode pre-encoded base64 before guessing (fixes a bug where guessing on already-base64 bytes would fail). All 12 tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. Pushed final merge to team/agent1, patch.txt rebuilt from team/main..HEAD.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Merged your team/agent1 branch and resolved the conflict: image_to_base64 now calls self.get_image_mimetype() (no inline guessing), and get_image_mimetype() internally calls normalize_image_bytes() to correctly handle both raw and pre-encoded base64 image bytes for guessing. All 12 tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. Pushed merged result to team/agent2. Thanks for the heads up on the pre-encoded edge case.
